title: Tighten small AV drift correction and fix demo3 shuffle toggle parity
summary: 0.6.60 keeps the existing one-second periodic AV sync cadence, lowers the correction threshold to 0.05 seconds, arms an immediate first post-seek sync check, and switches the demo3 shuffle checkbox over to a true toggle transition so it can disable random playback as well as enable it.
changes: Periodic AV sync still checks at one-second cadence, but now intervenes when drift reaches 0.05 seconds instead of 0.20 seconds.
The VideoPlayer seek-completed hook now arms the next AV sync check immediately instead of waiting a full second after scrub or skip resume.
The demo3 shuffle checkbox now fires a single Player Random Toggle action on state transition instead of separate on and off edge actions.
reasoning: The remaining sync issue was small enough to slip under the previous 0.20 second correction threshold, especially right after seek or skip resume. Separately, the shuffle checkbox bug was a scene control parity issue: the player default and natural ordering were already right, but the demo scene toggle could enable random playback without reliably disabling it again.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Small audio/video drift should correct sooner without changing the one-second sync cadence.
Skip or scrub resume should not leave a lingering 100-200ms offset before the first correction opportunity.
The demo3 shuffle checkbox should now toggle random playback off as well as on.
knownIssues: This slice does not change image scrub transport, movie joystick transport, or slideshow behavior.
This slice does not change right-stick volume or deferred gaze work.
verification: The seekCompleted handler now arms nextAudioVideoSyncCheckTime to Time.unscaledTime in FASyncRuntime.Player.cs.
Build-PlayerDemoScene.ps1 now wires checkbox_shuffle through a single Player Random Toggle transition action.
